content.text:
The code is in a coherent state. Here's what changed since the last commit (`Create new sessions with embedded terminal windows.`):

**Unstaged changes (3 files):**

1. **`SessionDetail.tsx`** — Two additions:
   - `hideTerminal` flag from `location.state` (used by `SpawnSessionScreen` so the terminal doesn't appear twice)
   - 500ms polling loop when `session.isLive` is true, so live sessions update faster than the server's 2s broadcast

2. **`SpawnSessionScreen.tsx`** — New file (untracked), the full spawn tab UI

3. **Minor changes** in `Shell.tsx`, `TabsContext.tsx`, `ActiveSessions.tsx`, `EmbeddedTerminal.tsx`, `SessionRow.tsx`, `server.ts` — all supporting the spawn-session feature

Everything looks complete and consistent. What do you want to work on next?

message.content: Let's persist the open tabs across sessions. When i exit the app and reopen it, i should be able to see the same tabs that i had open before.

content.content: /Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer/app/src/main/index.ts
/Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer/app/src/preload/index.ts

content.text: The simplest approach: use `localStorage` in the renderer to persist tab state. No need to touch Electron main/preload. I'll skip `spawn` tabs on restore (their PTY is gone), and always include the `home` tab.
Let me also check how `TabsProvider` is called:

content.content: /Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer/app/src/renderer/src/main.tsx

1→import React from "react";
     2→import ReactDOM from "react-dom/client";
     3→import { MantineProvider, createTheme, localStorageColorSchemeManager } from "@mantine/core";
     4→import "@mantine/core/styles.css";
     5→import App from "./App";
     6→
     7→const colorSchemeManager = localStorageColorSchemeManager({ key: "gossamer-color-scheme" });
     8→
     9→const theme = createTheme({
    10→  primaryColor: "indigo",
    11→  fontFamily: '-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif',
    12→  fontFamilyMonospace: 'ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, "SF Mono", Menlo, Consolas, "Liberation Mono", monospace',
    13→  colors: {
    14→    dark: [
    15→      "#e6edf3", // [0] primary text
    16→      "#8b949e", // [1] muted text

    18→      "#484f58", // [3]

    25→    ],
    26→  },
    27→});
    28→

    32→      <App />
    33→    </MantineProvider>
    34→  </React.StrictMode>
    35→);

content.text:
Now I have everything I need. The plan:

- Store tabs in `localStorage` on every change (skip `spawn` tabs — their PTY is gone)
- On startup, read from `localStorage` to restore tabs
- Only persist/restore in the **main window** (when no `?tab=` URL param); dedicated windows always use their specific tab

All changes are in `TabsContext.tsx`:

content.content: The file /Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer/app/src/renderer/src/TabsContext.tsx has been updated successfully.

lines: | { id: string; type: "branchLog"; localPath: string; branch: string; repoName: string | null; title: string }
| { id: string; type: "spawn"; cwd: string; command: string; title: string; spawnedAt: number };
+  | { id: string; type: "session"; sessionId: string; title: string }
+  | { id: string; type: "branchLog"; localPath: string; branch: string; repoName: string | null; title: string };
+function loadPersistedState(): { tabs: Tab[]; activeTabId: string } | null {
+    const { tabs, activeTabId } = JSON.parse(raw) as { tabs: PersistedTab[]; activeTabId: string };
+    const restoredActiveId = valid.find((t) => t.id === activeTabId) ? activeTabId : "home";
+    return { tabs: [HOME_TAB, ...valid] as Tab[], activeTabId: restoredActiveId };

lastPrompt: Let's persist the open tabs across sessions. When i exit the app and reopen it, i should be able to see the same tabs that i had open before.
